mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. The signal is active whenever UNRATE decreases from its previous level, regardless of the level.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has been declining from its post-crisis peak of 10.0% in October 2009 to 7.2% as of June 2014
failure_condition: If the month-over-month change in UNRATE is negative (falling) and the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in fewer than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the following 20 trading days. The trigger is a discrete acceleration threshold.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth >1% and the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both the labor market (falling UNRATE) and consumer demand (rising RSAFS) indicates a synchronized expansion that reinforces corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The joint signal is more powerful than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases month-over-month and RSAFS increases month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: If the joint condition (UNRATE declining and RSAFS rising) occurs and the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not positive at a 95% confidence level (one-sided t-test), or if the rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.03 over a 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above the zero lower bound of 0.25% signals the start of monetary policy normalization, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon in a post-crisis normalization regime.
failure_condition: If after the first occurrence of FEDFUNDS >0.25% in the sample, the 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of subsequent instances, or if the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings and investor sentiment, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon. The signal is always active and does not require any threshold condition.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month change in INDPRO is positive and the 5-day S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 700 thousand units per month signals a strengthening housing market that stimulates related industries and consumer wealth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The threshold is based on historical post-recession recovery levels.
failure_condition: If HOUST >700 and the 20-day S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than 40% of such events over any 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of improving labor market (UNRATE falling by at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month) and persistently accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25%) provides a powerful signal for sustained economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by 0.2pp or more month-over-month and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs and the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or if more than 40% of individual instances show negative returns, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI YoY increase below 3%), a moderate rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) signals strengthening demand and pricing power for corporations, which boosts earnings expectations and equity prices over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism is conditional on the inflation regime and does not require a trigger on the proxy itself.
failure_condition: If the low-inflation regime is active (CPI YoY < 3%) and a month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL is positive, but the 20-day S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than 50% of such cases over a 3-year period, reject the hypothesis.
